channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Pakistan claims to have killed at least 70 militants in strikes along Afghan border
item.description: Pakistan's military killed at least 70 militants in strikes along the border with Afghanistan early Sunday, the deputy interior minister said.

item.title: University students hold new protests in Iran around memorials for those killed
item.description: Iran's state news agency said students protested at five universities in the capital, Tehran, and one in the city of Mashhad on Sunday.

item.title: Mexican army kills leader of Jalisco New Generation Cartel, official says
item.description: The Mexican army killed the leader of the powerful Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, "El Mencho," in an operation Sunda...

item.title: Golden again! U.S. tops Canada to win 1st men's hockey gold since 'Miracle on Ice'
item.description: The U.S. won its first Olympic gold in 46 years in a 2-1 overtime thriller over rival Canada. A brilliant performance by U.S. goaltender Connor Helleb...

item.title: Ukraine's combat amputees cling to hope as a weapon of war
item.description: Along with a growing number of war-wounded amputees, Mykhailo Varvarych and Iryna Botvynska are navigating an altered destiny after Varvarych lost bot...

item.title: Armed man shot and killed after entering secure perimeter of Mar-a-Lago, Secret Service says
item.description: Trump was not there but was at the White House in Washington.

item.title: Violence erupts after Mexican cartel leader "El Mencho" killed in federal operation
item.description: Mexico's Ministry of Defense security forces killed the leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, known as "El Me...

item.title: Greenland says 'no thanks' to Trump US hospital boat
item.description: Greenland's PM reminded Trump of its free healthcare, after Trump said he was sending a boat to aid people who were allegedly "not being taken care of...

item.title: Maps show winter storm forecast bring snow, blizzard conditions to East Coast
item.description: Blizzard warnings were issued Saturday for New York City, New Jersey, southern New England and coastal communities along the East Coast.

item.title: Trump curious why Iran has not 'capitulated', US envoy Witkoff says
item.description: US envoy Steve Witkoff says the president is puzzled why Iran has not yet compromised in the face of a major American military build-up nearby.

item.title: Mexico's most wanted drug lord 'El Mencho' killed in military operation
item.description: Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as "El Mencho", headed one of Mexico's most powerful drug cartels.

item.title: Bones of St Francis of Assisi go on public display in Italy
item.description: The remains of Italy's patron saint have only been seen in public once before - for one day in 1978.

item.title: Armed man killed after entering secure perimeter of Trump's residence, Secret Service says
item.description: The suspect was carrying a shotgun and fuel can when he was killed, officers said. Trump was in Washington DC at the time.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (Feb. 22)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Blizzard warnings cascade across East Coast with winter storm’s approach
NASA to start slow-moving process returning moon rocket to hangar this week
item.description: The space agency said Sunday it's targeting Tuesday for the slow, four-mile trek across Kennedy Space Center​, weather permitting.

item.title: 2/22: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the Supreme Court struck a blow to President Trump's tariffs, Margaret Brennan spoke to U.S. ...

item.title: TSA says PreCheck still operational after previous announcement of suspension during funding fight
